mainText: Good morning. Here is your curated AI briefing for Thursday, July 23, 2026. The most important story today is the $10.3 billion valuation of AI chip startup Etched. Founded by three Harvard dropouts in 2022, the company recently closed a $300 million Series C funding round led by Sequoia. This valuation is notable because it has doubled in just seven months, marking what the company claims is the highest valuation ever for a Sequoia-led Series C. Etched is developing specialized hardware designed to run transformer-based models—the architecture behind systems like ChatGPT—as well as newer designs like Mamba. The startup’s core technical strategy focuses on two stages of inference, which is the process of generating output after a user submits a prompt. For the prefill stage, Etched designed a chip that runs at a lower voltage to generate less heat and pack in more transistors. For the decode stage, it developed an interconnect technology called cluster scale memory. While the company faces skepticism regarding its ability to compete with established giants like Nvidia, it reports having booked $1 billion in orders. Access to its systems remains limited to investors and early customers who have participated in private demonstrations. In other major developments, Nvidia is expanding its presence in space through a partnership with the robotics startup Lunar Outpost. The startup announced that its next moon rover will use Nvidia Jetson chips to manage its LiDAR system, marking a potential milestone as the first GPU on the lunar surface. The project is part of a broader push by NASA to use private robotics for lunar exploration. Operating hardware in space presents significant technical challenges, including extreme temperature fluctuations and cosmic radiation. Nvidia also recently opened an AI supercomputer system at the Naval Postgraduate School in Monterey, California. The installation, which features the DGX GB300 system, is intended to provide graduate students and faculty with on-premises computing power for research in areas such as weather prediction, cybersecurity, and disaster response. In the pharmaceutical industry, AI is increasingly integrated into the design of biologic medicines. According to reporting from earlier this week, companies like AstraZeneca are utilizing AI to navigate complex molecular design tasks. By using machine learning models to prioritize candidate molecules, researchers can focus lab efforts on options most likely to succeed, theoretically shortening development timelines by up to 50 percent. Success in this field relies heavily on "data moats," or the use of proprietary datasets, including molecular structures and safety profiles, to train these specialized models. Elsewhere in the ecosystem, Hugging Face announced a new method for running 4-bit diffusion inference directly within its Diffusers library. The tool, called Nunchaku Lite, allows users to load compressed models without requiring local custom compilation or separate inference engines. This method uses a quantization technique called SVDQuant to reduce memory usage, allowing 1024-by-1024 image generation to occur with roughly half the memory required by standard methods. Meanwhile, monday.com is scaling its internal agentic AI systems—referred to as "AI Teammates"—using Amazon Bedrock. The company uses these agents to perform engineering tasks, such as picking up tickets and shipping features, by integrating them directly into existing workflows like Slack and GitHub. Additionally, OpenAI released reports from various news organizations detailing how they are using AI tools to assist in journalism. Outlets including The Associated Press and The Philadelphia Inquirer are using these technologies to assist with tasks such as verifying imagery, scanning government datasets for stories, and summarizing transcripts from public meetings. Nvidia’s cloud gaming service, GeForce NOW, is also expanding its library this week with the addition of titles like Path of Exile: Curse of the Allflame and various classic Capcom games. Looking ahead, market observers will be watching to see how successfully Etched transitions from prototype testing to mass production and delivery of its rack systems. The industry will also monitor whether the deployment of GPUs on the lunar surface helps Lunar Outpost meet its ambitious goals for establishing long-term autonomous operations on the moon. That is your curated AI briefing for today.
